# 🤝 Contributing to rickordbox

First off, thank you for considering contributing to rickordbox! 🎉

## 📋 Table of Contents

- [Code of Conduct](#-code-of-conduct)
- [Getting Started](#-getting-started)
- [How to Contribute](#-how-to-contribute)
- [Development Setup](#-development-setup)
- [Style Guidelines](#-style-guidelines)
- [Pull Request Process](#-pull-request-process)

## 💖 Code of Conduct

Be kind, be respectful, and help make this community welcoming for everyone. We're all here because we love music! 🎵

## 🚀 Getting Started

1. Fork the repository
2. Clone your fork: `git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/rickordbox.git`
3. Create a branch: `git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature`
4. Make your changes
5. Push and open a PR!

## 🛠️ How to Contribute

### 🐛 Bug Reports

Found a bug? Please open an issue with:
- Clear description of the problem
- Steps to reproduce
- Expected vs actual behavior
- Browser and OS information

### 💡 Feature Requests

Have an idea? We'd love to hear it! Open an issue with:
- Description of the feature
- Why it would be useful
- Any implementation ideas

### 🔧 Code Contributions

- Check existing issues for things to work on
- Look for `good first issue` labels for beginner-friendly tasks
- Feel free to ask questions in issues!

## 💻 Development Setup

```bash
# Install dependencies
npm install

# Start dev server
npm run dev

# Run tests
npm test

# Run linter
npm run lint
```

## 🎨 Style Guidelines

- **TypeScript**: Use strict types, avoid `any`
- **React**: Functional components with hooks
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS classes
- **Formatting**: Prettier (run before committing)

## 📤 Pull Request Process

1. ✅ Ensure all tests pass
2. ✅ Update documentation if needed
3. ✅ Write a clear PR description
4. ✅ Link related issues
5. ✅ Request review from maintainers

---

*Happy coding!* 🎧✨
